title: Iran Shot Down a US Apache Near Hormuz. Now Both Sides Are Striking.
script: Iran shot down who?! On June 9, an Iranian missile hit a US Army AH-64 Apache helicopter patrolling near the Strait of Hormuz. Both pilots were rescued by drone boat — they survived. But the US wasn't done. CENTCOM launched airstrikes on Iranian air defense systems, radar sites, and ground control stations along the southern coast of Iran. Iran then fired back — targeting US military bases in Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan with drones and missiles. Trump said the US will continue striking Iran. It's the most direct exchange of fire between the 2 nations in years. A ceasefire was officially in place but has now suffered multiple violations. All this because of 1 helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz — the world's most critical oil lane. Will Iran and the US find a way back to the ceasefire, or is this spiraling?
